In this case, an UPDATE on ztfre_layout with "SET min_stock = 0" should work too, without selecting the data beforehand. Check the documentation here.

entry.title: Re: Complete Table and Method to attain Table Number for...
entry.content:
In SAP ERP, SE16N (or SE16) is a transaction used for table display and data exploration. While there isn't a single predefined "complete list" of tables available for SE16N, you can access the relevant tables for a specific transaction code (T-code) or business process in a few ways. Here's how to look up tables for any particular T-code, such as VK11 (Condition Records):

Finding Tables for a Specific T-code (e.g., VK11 for Condition Records):

  1. Use the Technical Information (F1 Help):

    • Navigate to the transaction or screen you're interested in, such as VK11.
    • Once in the transaction, press F1 to get the field help for the relevant field or screen.
    • Click on the Technical Information button (or icon). This will display the underlying table names and field names for the current screen or field.
    • The Table/View field in the Technical Information window will give you the name of the table(s) used for that specific field or screen.

  2. Using Transaction SE11 (ABAP Dictionary):

    • You can also search for the related tables using SE11, the ABAP Dictionary transaction.
    • In SE11, you can search for Database Tables.
      • Enter the relevant name or the part of the name (like KONV for Condition Records) to identify the tables used in the process.
      • For condition records (VK11), the main table is KONV, which stores condition records, and KONP, which stores the condition values.

  3. Using the "Where-Used List" in SE11:

    • If you know a specific object or table (e.g., KONV for Condition Records), you can search for its Where-Used List.
    • In SE11, go to Where-Used List and enter the table name, such as KONV.
    • This will show you all the places where this table is used, including the T-codes or programs that interact with it.

  4. Explore the Transaction Code via the Menu Path:

    • Each transaction code (T-code) corresponds to certain programs, function modules, and tables.
    • In some cases, you can find the relevant tables used for a T-code by reviewing the menu path and technical objects in SE93 (Customizing for Tcodes) or ST03N (Workload Analysis).

Example for VK11 (Condition Records):

  • KONV: This is the main table for condition records in SAP, which contains condition values.
  • KONP: Contains condition records for pricing.
  • AUSP: Stores attributes for condition records.
  • You can also explore views like V_002 (View for pricing conditions) or check tables related to pricing procedures in T685 for condition types and related pricing information.

Using SE16N Directly:

  • In SE16N, you can enter the table name directly (for example, KONV, KONP, or AUSP) to explore the data.
  • If you want to see related tables, look for foreign key relationships in SE11 (ABAP Dictionary) or technical information from the field help.

General Steps for Finding Tables in SE16N:

  1. Open SE16N.
  2. Enter a known table name (e.g., KONV for condition records).
  3. You can also use Table joins or explore views for more complex scenarios.

Summary:

  1. F1 Help > Technical Info for a specific field in any T-code.
  2. Use SE11 to search for relevant tables related to a T-code or business process.
  3. Use Where-Used List to find all places where a table is referenced.
  4. Check Table/Field Relationships and Views for comprehensive data access.

By following these steps, you can find the relevant tables for any T-code, like VK11 for condition records, and then explore them further in SE16N.

entry.title: Re: CJ88 unable to settle revenue correctly
entry.content:
In SAP ERP, the issue you're facing with CJ88 not posting revenue correctly could be linked to the way results analysis (RA) is configured, particularly how periodic amounts are handled.

Here are a few things to consider:

  1. Periodic vs. Cumulative Revenue Posting: In CJ88, when you execute settlement for a project, the system usually settles the cumulative results analysis amounts, which reflect all revenue postings up to the current period. However, if the system is posting the amount for only the current period, it might be due to the configuration in the RA or settlement rules.

    • Check the Results Analysis (RA) Method used in the project. Ensure that it is configured to post the correct cumulative or periodic amounts. Some RA methods may be set to calculate on a cumulative basis, while others may use periodic amounts, depending on how the settlement profiles and the result analysis strategy are set up.

  2. Settlement Rule Configuration: Go to the project settlement rule (Transaction: CJ02) and check the configuration for the settlement type. If it’s set to "Revenue" for settlement, verify that the settlement parameters (such as period selection) align with what you're expecting for the revenue amounts.

  3. RA/Settlement Period Selection: Check the result analysis and settlement period settings. If the system is considering the wrong period for posting, it could cause discrepancies between what is shown in KKA2 and what gets posted in CJ88. You may need to check if there is a mismatch between the revenue in period 10 and the system’s RA calculation, possibly influenced by the "periodic" setting.

  4. RA Line Items Review: Use transaction KKA2 to analyze the result items. You might need to check if there is a variance between the cumulative and periodic amounts that the system is considering for the settlement. This could indicate that the calculation is not matching the expected settlement structure.

  5. Check for Posting Logs: If you have configured posting control in the project system or revenue postings, check if there are any errors or warnings related to the settlement in the logs of transaction CJ88. There may be a restriction or configuration causing only the periodic amount to be posted, instead of the cumulative amount.

To summarize:

  • Verify the RA method and settings to ensure it is not set to only post periodic amounts.
  • Check the settlement rule and configuration to ensure the correct amounts are being used in the settlement transaction.
  • Inspect the KKA2 analysis for periodic and cumulative data to confirm that the amounts match the expectations for the settlement.

entry.title: Re: Workflow for a Purchase order with different plants i...
entry.content:
To handle this scenario in SAP Public Cloud for a Purchase Order (PO) workflow where the approver depends on whether the PO contains a single plant or multiple plants, 

Steps to Implement:

  1. Define Workflow Conditions Based on Plant Count:

    • You need to define a condition that checks whether the PO contains a single plant (i.e., one plant in the line items) or multiple plants. This can be achieved by creating a condition based on the number of unique plants in the PO line items.

  2. Customizing the Workflow:

    • Use Custom Conditions in Workflow: You can leverage custom conditions in the workflow to count the distinct plants in the PO. For example:
      • In the workflow step condition, define a custom function or rule to count the distinct plants in the PO lines.
      • If the count of distinct plants is 1 (only I400), then set the approver as L1: Local Accountant.
      • If the count of distinct plants is greater than 1 (I400 & I403), set the approver as L1: Central Finance.

  3. Create Custom Business Rules:

    • In SAP Public Cloud, Business Rules Framework (BRF+) can be used to define custom logic. You could create a business rule to evaluate the line items of the PO and determine the number of unique plants.
    • Once the number of unique plants is identified, assign the appropriate workflow step.

  4. Defining Approver Assignment:

    • In your workflow, define a step where the approver is assigned based on the rule you created above:
      • For Scenario 1 (single plant), assign approver XYZ as L1: Local Accountant.
      • For Scenario 2 (multiple plants), assign approver ABC as L1: Central Finance.

Example of Logic:

  1. Step Condition:

    • Create a custom step condition that evaluates the PO line items. For example, you can use a script or a BRF+ rule to count the number of distinct plants in the PO items.

  2. Branching Logic:

    • If the number of unique plants equals 1, assign approver XYZ (Local Accountant).
    • If the number of unique plants is greater than 1, assign approver ABC (Central Finance).

Configuring in SAP Public Cloud:

  1. Transaction or Application in Public Cloud: You will likely use the Manage Workflows application in SAP Public Cloud to configure the workflow steps and conditions.

    • When creating the workflow, use the conditions and steps based on the above logic.
    • The workflow should trigger based on the number of unique plants present in the PO.

  2. Testing the Workflow:

    • After setting up the conditions and steps, thoroughly test the workflow with different scenarios (PO with single plant vs. multiple plants) to ensure the correct approvers are assigned.

entry.title: Re: IS Retail: Customer master table KNVV is missing
entry.content:
the issue regarding missing entries in the KNVV table while processing IDOCs in SAP IS-Retail! This explanation will certainly help others who face similar challenges in the future. 

Issue:

While processing IDOCs, you encountered an error: "Customer master table KNVV is missing."

Root Cause:
The division assigned to the site (e.g., JH) was not available in the KNVV table for the respective customer, causing the IDOC processing to fail.

Solution Steps:

  1. Verify Existing Data in KNVV Table:

    • Use SE16N or SE11 to check the KNVV table for the customer and verify if the relevant Sales Area (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division) exists.

  2. Check the Site’s Division:

    • Use T-Code WB03 to display the site and identify its assigned Division.

  3. Update Customer Sales Area Data:

    • Use T-Code XD02:
      • Enter the Customer Number.
      • Add the missing Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, and Division.
      • Click the Create icon to add these details.

  4. Confirm Changes in KNVV:

    • Recheck the KNVV table to ensure the new entries for the relevant Sales Area are created successfully.

  5. Reprocess IDOCs:

    • Use T-Code BD87 to reprocess the failed IDOCs.
    • Verify successful processing.

  6. Alternative T-Code for Confirmation:

    • You can also use T-Code WPER to confirm successful processing of IDOCs.

Note:

  • Ensure that the Sales Area updates align with the organizational structure and processes in SAP IS-Retail.
  • Regular checks for master data consistency can prevent such issues from recurring.

entry.title: Meaningless "posted a topic/replied to topic", instead use "question", "discussion", "blog article"
entry.content:
I see in my Notification feed questions, discussions and blog posts which are all tagged "posted a new topic" or "replied to topic", and I need to look at the picture to know if it's a question, a blog post or even a discussion. It has taken me months to change my habit from going from the text to the picture, in fact I have to look at both parts, the kind of topic, and the topic title. Why not having the whole information in the text? Proposing to replace "topic" with either "question", "discussion" or "blog article".

Here, it's a question:

It's a blog post:

It's a discussion:

Surprisingly, "topic" is not used in "commented on blog article":

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Budget Transfer Timing for STO Transactions
entry.content:
Hello All,

I hope this message finds you well.

I have a query regarding our current process for transferring materials between projects using Stock Transport Orders (STOs) within our multiple SAP plants. When we transfer materials from Project A to Project B, the associated budget amount is immediately debited from Project A and credited to Project B, even if the material is still in transit or the Goods Receipt Notice (GRN) is pending.

This early budget transfer complicates our financial tracking, as the funds are shifted before the materials are officially received at Plant B.

Is there a solution or configuration within SAP that would allow us to withhold the budget transfer until the materials have been received and verified at Plant B? Your insights on optimizing this process would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: New Live Session: Starting with AI in SAP Customer Experience Solutions
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community! I’m back (again) writing about some SAP CX Product Learning news!

Did you know that there a a lot of Use Cases for Artificial Intelligence in SAP Customer Experience solutions?

Some of them rely on Predictive AI like Lead Scoring in SAP Sales Cloud, Others are build on top of Generative AI like Personalized Product descriptions for SAP Commerce Cloud, Also you can use embedded AI in the solution or leverage the CX AI Toolkit to promote Conversational AI utilization based on Joule.

At first I was also a bit overwhelmed about this AI topic 😰, so get into it 🤓, after a digesting it 🐳, I have prepared 👨‍🍳 the Starting with AI in SAP Customer Experience Solutions Live Session, so that you can get in one hour quite an overview of the AI topic.

During this live session, we'll start covering briefly the SAP Business AI strategy, and the particular strategy for CX. Later, we'll cover most relevant uses cases for AI across the whole SAP Customer Experience solutions portfolio with explanations but also live demos performed on the systems. We finalize with more resources for you to continue learning about the topic.

Let’s explore together AI use cases for SAP CX Solutions!

This session will be held twice per quarter during 2025, and next edition is on December 2024. and as I’ve said, the live session is already available on SAP Learning. You will need to have a subscription in order to register for this live session there or watch the recording of a previous edition. 

For better understanding of this live session’s content, it’s recommended you know already a bit about the different SAP Customer Experience Solutions before attending to this informative live session. No previous AI knowledge is required.

Awesome! thanks for reading until the end of this post🎆! I’ll return very soon with new topics, but until then, please share any comments or suggestions below.

entry.title: Hi Please help me with error. G/L account 4000 cannot be used (please correct)Message no. ME045
entry.content:
G/L account 4000 cannot be used (please correct)

Message no. ME045

Diagnosis

Comparison of the field selection strings from the G/L account 4000 and the account assignment category K reveals that there is an incompatible combination of field selections for the field selection group 'Cost center'.

The G/L account field selection is stored in table T004F; that for the account assignment is stored in table T162K.

Procedure

Please enter a correct G/L account.

entry.title: Closing Zero-Balance Time Accounts to Hide from Employee Profiles Using SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Managing time accounts in SuccessFactors Time Management can sometimes require a customized approach, especially when the goal is to improve user experience by hiding time accounts with zero balances. This blog discusses a custom integration solution using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) to close time accounts with zero balance, effectively hiding them from employees’ profiles when applying for absences.

Business Requirement

In our organization, there was a need to remove zero-balance time accounts from employee profiles to streamline the absence application process. While exploring options within SuccessFactors, we found no out-of-the-box solution to achieve this. As a result, we developed a custom integration to close these zero-balance time accounts using SAP Cloud Integration.

Key Considerations

Before implementing this solution, it’s essential to clarify the following requirements with the business stakeholders:

  1. Define Specific Time Accounts: Determine which time account types need to be closed at zero balance, so only these accounts are included in the interface configuration.

  2. Exclude Future Postings: The interface should close only those time accounts with a zero balance as of today’s date, ignoring any future-dated absences.

  3. Avoid Future-Dated Time Accounts: Exclude any time accounts with a future start date (e.g., those starting on January 1, 2025) from the interface, ensuring only currently active time accounts are included.

Solution Design: SAP Cloud Integration Flow

 

The following steps outline the integration flow (iFlow) developed to accomplish the closure of zero-balance time accounts in SuccessFactors.

Step 1: Set Properties

  • Content Modifier: Set the property “todaydate” to capture the current date.

 

Step 2: Retrieve Time Account Data

  • Request-Reply: Fetch time account data from SuccessFactors, filtering for active accounts where the booking start date is on or before today’s date.

 

Step 3: Filter Applicable Time Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter_Time_Accounts: This script is designed to filter only those time accounts that need to be closed when their balance is zero.

 

Step 4: Exclude Future-Dated Absences

  • XSLT Script - Exclude Future Dated Bookings: Excludes any time accounts with future-dated bookings, ensuring that only accounts with past or current dates are processed.

 

Step 5: Calculate Available Balance

  • XSLT Script - Calculate Available Balance: This script calculates the remaining balance for each time account. For example, if an employee has 16 hours and uses two 8-hour absences, the balance would reach zero (16 - 8 - 8 = 0).

 

Step 6: Filter Zero-Balance Accounts

  • XSLT Script - Filter Zero Balance: This script filters accounts with a zero balance, extracting relevant data fields like external code, user ID, and setting “accountclosed” to true.

 

Step 7: Split Records Per Time Account

  • Splitter: Splits the records by each time account to individually send closure details to SuccessFactors.

 

Step 8: Update SuccessFactors

  • Request-Reply: Posts the updated time account details back to SuccessFactors, closing each zero-balance account in the system.

 

Outcome in SuccessFactors

  • Before: Employees could see two time accounts with zero balances.

 

  • After: The integration closes only the active zero-balance time account (e.g., the current year’s account) while leaving future-dated accounts (e.g., Time Account 2025) unaffected.

 

 

This solution effectively hides zero-balance time accounts from employees, enhancing the user experience and ensuring they see only relevant information in their profiles. Through SAP Cloud Integration, organizations can better manage employee time accounts, keeping the interface clean and intuitive.

#Packing loss

#Dispensing loss

entry.title: Help Needed: Error Uploading Salary Journal Entries from Excel to SAP Public Cloud via SOAP API
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on a project to upload salary journal entries from an Excel sheet to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using a SOAP API.

I have created a Fiori app that:

  1. Uploads and processes Excel files using the XLSX library.
  2. Generates a SOAP request XML from the processed data.
  3. Sends the SOAP request to a CAP service, which then forwards the request to the SOAP API via a Destination configured in SAP BTP.

Here are the key components of my implementation:

View.xml:

mvc:View
    controllerName="project1.controller.View1"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:unified="sap.ui.unified">
    <Page id="page" title="Excel Upload Example">
        <content>
            <VBox id="vbox" class="sapUiSmallMargin">
                <Label id="L1" text="Upload Excel File" />
                <Panel id ="p1">
                    <content>
                        <unified:FileUploader
                            id="excelUploader"
                            name="myFileUpload"
                            width="400px"
                            placeholder="Choose an Excel file"
                            change="onFileChange" />
                    </content>
                </Panel>
                <Button id="btn" text="Process and Export" press="onProcessAndExport" />
               </VBox>
        </content>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
controller.js:
sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
    "sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel",
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
 
], function (Controller, JSONModel, MessageToast ) {
    "use strict";
    return Controller.extend("project1.controller.View1", {
        onInit: function () {
            // Initialize the model for the table
            var oModel = new JSONModel({ data: [] });
            this.getView().setModel(oModel, "excelData");

        },

        onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
            var oFile = oEvent.getParameter("files")[0];
            if (oFile && window.FileReader) {
                var reader = new FileReader();
                reader.onload = function (e) {
                    var data = new Uint8Array(e.target.result);
                    var binaryString = '';
                    for (var i = 0; i < data.byteLength; i++) {
                        binaryString += String.fromCharCode(data[i]);
                    }

                    // Check if XLSX library is loaded
                    if (typeof XLSX === "undefined") {
                        MessageToast.show("XLSX library not loaded.");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Read the workbook
                    try {
                        var workbook = XLSX.read(binaryString, { type: 'binary' });
                        var sheetName = workbook.SheetNames[0];
                        var jsonSheet = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_json(workbook.Sheets[sheetName]);

                        // Set the data model
                        var oModel = this.getView().getModel("excelData");
                        oModel.setProperty("/data", jsonSheet);

                        MessageToast.show("Excel uploaded successfully!");
                    } catch (error) {
                        MessageToast.show("Error reading Excel file: " + error.message);
                    }
                }.bind(this);

                // Read as ArrayBuffer
                reader.readAsArrayBuffer(oFile);
            } else {
                MessageToast.show("No file selected or FileReader not supported.");
            }
        },

        onProcessAndExport: function () {
            var oModel = this.getView().getModel("excelData");
            var data = oModel.getProperty("/data");

            MessageToast.show("Processing data...");

            // Generate SOAP XML
            var soapXml = this.createSoapRequest(data);

            // Send the SOAP request
            this.callSendSoapRequestToCAP(soapXml);
        },

       
     
        createSoapRequest: function (data) {
            const creationDateTime = new Date().toISOString();
            const creationDate = creationDateTime.split('T')[0]; // Get date in YYYY-MM-DD format
            let envelope = `<soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:sfin="http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN">
                <soapenv:Header/>
                <soapenv:Body>
                    <sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>
                        <MessageHeader>
                            <ID>MSG_${creationDate}</ID>
                            <CreationDateTime>${creationDateTime}</CreationDateTime>
                        </MessageHeader>`;

            // Group data by Journal Entry (if needed)
            const journalEntries = {}; // Store entries grouped by Document Reference ID

            data.forEach(entry => {
                const docRefId = entry.DocumentReferenceID; // Use this to group items

                if (!journalEntries[docRefId]) {
                    journalEntries[docRefId] = [];
                }

                // Push each entry to the corresponding Journal Entry group
                journalEntries[docRefId].push(entry);
            });

            // Loop through each journal entry and create JournalEntryCreateRequest
            Object.keys(journalEntries).forEach((docRefId, index) => {
                const entries = journalEntries[docRefId];

                const formatDate = (dateValue) => {
                    // Check if date is in Excel serial format (e.g., 45598)
                    if (typeof dateValue === "number") {
                        const excelBaseDate = new Date(Date.UTC(1899, 11, 30));
                        const dateObj = new Date(excelBaseDate.getTime() + dateValue * 86400000);
                        return dateObj.toISOString().split('T')[0];
                    }

                    // If date is a string, parse it and format it as YYYY-MM-DD
                    const dateObj = new Date(dateValue);
                    const year = dateObj.getFullYear();
                    const month = String(dateObj.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
                    const day = String(dateObj.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');

                    return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
                };

                const documentDate = formatDate(entries[0].DocumentDate);
                const postingDate = formatDate(entries[0].PostingDate);

                envelope += `
                    <JournalEntryCreateRequest>
                        <MessageHeader>
                            <ID>SUB_MSG_${creationDate}${index + 1}</ID>
                            <CreationDateTime>${creationDateTime}</CreationDateTime>
                        </MessageHeader>
                        <JournalEntry>
                            <OriginalReferenceDocumentType>${entries[0].OriginalReferenceDocumentType}</OriginalReferenceDocumentType>
                            <OriginalReferenceDocument/>
                            <OriginalReferenceDocumentLogicalSystem/>
                            <BusinessTransactionType>${entries[0].BusinessTransactionType}</BusinessTransactionType>
                            <AccountingDocumentType>${entries[0].AccountingDocumentType}</AccountingDocumentType>
                            <DocumentReferenceID>${docRefId}</DocumentReferenceID>
                            <DocumentHeaderText>${entries[0].DocumentHeaderText}</DocumentHeaderText>
                            <CreatedByUser>CC0000000001</CreatedByUser>
                            <CompanyCode>${entries[0].CompanyCode}</CompanyCode>
                            <DocumentDate>${documentDate}</DocumentDate>
                            <PostingDate>${postingDate}</PostingDate>
                            <Reference1InDocumentHeader>12345678901234567890</Reference1InDocumentHeader>
                            <Reference2InDocumentHeader>1234567890</Reference2InDocumentHeader>`;

                // Add items for each entry related to this journal entry
                entries.forEach(item => {
                    envelope += `
                            <Item>
                                <ReferenceDocumentItem>${item.ReferenceDocumentItem}</ReferenceDocumentItem>
                                <GLAccount>${item.GLAccount}</GLAccount>
                                <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="INR">${item.AmountInTransactionCurrency}</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
                                <DebitCreditCode>${item.DebitCreditCode}</DebitCreditCode>
                                <DocumentItemText>${item.DocumentItemText}</DocumentItemText>
                                <AccountAssignment>
                                    <CostCenter>${item.CostCenter}</CostCenter>
                                </AccountAssignment>
                            </Item>`;
                });

                envelope += `
                        </JournalEntry>
                    </JournalEntryCreateRequest>`;
            });

            envelope += `
                    </sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>
                </soapenv:Body>
            </soapenv:Envelope>`;

            return envelope;
        },

        callSendSoapRequestToCAP: function (soapXml) {
            //var oService = this.getView().getModel();  // Get the service model
       
            // Ensure that you're invoking the action through the correct service model
            //oService.create("/readExcelData", { soapXml: soapXml }, {
              //  success: function (response) {
               //   MessageToast.show("SOAP request sent successfully!");
               //   console.log("Response from CAP: ", response);
               // },
               // error: function (error) {
               //   MessageToast.show("Error sending SOAP request: " + error.message);
               //   console.error("Error: ", error);
               // }
              //});


              var sUrl = "/odata/v4/excel/readExcelData"; // Adjust this to your CAPM service URL

              $.ajax({
                  url: sUrl,
                  method: "POST",
                  contentType: "application/xml", // Set the content type to XML
                  dataType: "xml",
                  data: soapXml, // Send the XML data
               
                  success: function (response) {
                      sap.m.MessageToast.show("Data submitted successfully!" , response);
                  },
                  error: function (error) {
                      sap.m.MessageToast.show("Error submitting data.");
                      console.error(error);
                  }
              });
        }
       
    });
});


------------------------------------------------------------------------------
srv --> .js file :
const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const path = require('path'); // For handling file paths
const XLSX = require('xlsx'); // For reading Excel files
const { executeHttpRequest } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client');
const { retrieveJwt } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/core');
const xmlParser = require('fast-xml-parser');
const xml2js = require('xml2js');

module.exports = cds.service.impl(async function (req) {
    // Define an action in the service to read Excel data
    this.on('readExcelData', async (req) => {
        const contentType = req.headers['content-type'];
        const soapXml= req.data.soapXml;
        //const jsonData = JSON.parse(req.data);  // Parse the string data into JSON object

        console.log("data:", soapXml);

            //const xmlData = req.data; // Raw XML data

            //console.log(xmlData);

        const { DestinationService } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/core');
        const axios = require('axios');

        //const soapXml = req.data.soapXml;

        // Retrieve destination from SAP BTP
        const destinationService = new DestinationService();
        const destination = await destinationService.getDestination('Qorix_COCKPIT'); // Use your destination name here

        // Now use the destination to make the API call
        let config = {
            method: 'post',
            url: destination.url,  // This is the URL from your destination
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/xml',
                'Authorization': 'Basic ' + destination.credentials, // Ensure that the correct credentials are used
                //'Cookie': 'sap-usercontext=sap-client=100'
            },
            data: soapXml,
        };

        try {
            const response = await axios.request(config);
            console.log('Response:', response.data);
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('Error:', error);
        }
    }

   

);
});

------------------------------------------------

 

srv --> .cds file:

service ExcelService {
    action readExcelData(soapXml: String) returns String;
}
 

----------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: Modification of PO Field
entry.content:
Read first How to extend the number range from 10 digits to 12-16 digits for Purchase order?  (changing domain length considered as a modification, many dynpro to check)

You could also consider keeping the order number as it is, treat it as an internal number, and create a custom field at header level to record your own external number, fill it in at creation with your rule (from Enh MM06E004 and BAdI as ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST) then ensure that every document sent to the supplier (paper, EDI) uses the external number, and to provide at least an F4 search help to match the external number to the internal number (during GR, IR at least) but this will require some development and numerous tests, as you are no longer within the scope of SAP best practices.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

Else SAP Continuous Influence enables you to continuously suggest improvements directly to the product development teams.

entry.title: How to connect SAP BTP Destination to SAP CAP using node.js
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am looking for help related to connecting my cap application with destination created for another SAP SERVICE V2.

Below are steps followed:

1. Created a CAP project using template, with xsuaa,router and destination

2. In Package.json the configuration is maintained.

"cds":{

"requires" : {

"auth": "xsuaa"

"destinations": true

}

}

 

3. On deploying the cap project, a destination service is created bounded with the application. 

4. Now i have created a destination inside the destination service cretaed in the step 3.

5. Now, i want to consume the destination in my node.js to perform http actions.

 

 

The destination that is created is attached as screenshot. 

It is an odata v4 service with basic auth.

I am not able to connect to the destination  defined inside destination  service using cds.connect.to(<destinationname defined inside destination service)

Error:

Didn't find the a configuration  for 'cds.requires MEI_V2_DEV_New' in home/user/projects/CAP project.

 

Not able to conclude the exact issue.

 

 

#SAP BTP #CAPM  @Raja_Gupta @Kinsbrunner

entry.title: Re: Report on candidates hired through SF onboarding
entry.content:
Hi, the only way I can think of doing this is to use the Onboarding tables and not the EC tables (via Story Report). For example, you could use the Process table and filter by Hire Status = Hired. Naturally, one of the issues with getting the data from Onboarding is that eventually that data will be purged (~90 days depending on your particular system config).

I hope this helps.

Manuel G.

I was trying to replicate this issue and was able to successfully replicate to some extent.  Currently i have created a new Corporate IDP(Microsoft Entra) in the IAS system and the scenario works perfectly fine in case if the Identity Federation(User Store | User Access) is disabled. Also, ideally do we have to  enable identity federation(Only the Use Identity Authentication user store option) option or do we need to disable?

Can you please help with your comments pls!!

entry.title: Re: How to Add Fiscal Year Variants in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Verena,

I find myself in the same situation. My client has 10 company codes, all based in the USA. One of the company codes uses an April-March fiscal year, while the others follow a January-December fiscal year variant.

When we initially set up the S4HANA Public Cloud system, we selected the leading ledger (0L) and in the primary settings, chose the K4 (Jan-Dec) fiscal year variant, which we then saved. After adding all 10 company codes to the organizational structure, they defaulted to the K4 fiscal year variant. We have not added any other ledgers in to scope, since there are no specific reporting requirements.

Now, we would like to change the fiscal year variant for one of the company codes to April-March, but we're not seeing an option to make this change. This is in the Development system.

I would appreciate your insights and guidance on how to proceed.

entry.title: writing in same table without locking it
entry.content:
I am using sql anywhere 17, and get several connections concurrently trying to update the same row in a table.

Each connection per se does lot of calculations and at the end updates some specific rows in a table.

The problem is that sometimes different connections cause an update on the same row/s, so that they causes an deadlock, and no one of them can accomplish the job and commit.

Is there a simple way to solve such a problem withouth implementing isolation levels? since I dont have any experience with isolation levels.

entry.title: SAP PI Creating Dynamic URL on REST receiver adapter through UDF
entry.content:
We have an ECC to partner synchronous interface using PI 7.5. ECC sends a request to the partner through PI and the partner sends a response back. The ECC to the partner connection uses a REST receiver adapter. The interface is working fine except under some scenarios. We are using URL parameters to dynamically generate the URL.

The partner URL that PI is calling has the format   https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}?agencyLocationCode={ALC}&status={STATUS}&lastModifiedDateTime={LMDT}

This works just fine when ALC, STATUS and LMDT have values; so for example the following works

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234?agencyLocationCode=ALC1&status=ACTIVE&lastModifiedDateTime=12-31-2024

The problem arises when ALC, STATUS and LMDT do not have values; the URL that PI is generating looks like below

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234?agencyLocationCode=&status=&lastModifiedDateTime=

The application at the partner's side does not like this; they want us to use

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/id1234

So we introduced a UDF in the map to dynamically generate the URL and set the URL on the comm channel to  

https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}{ALC}{STATUS}{LMDT}

ALC, STATUS and LMDT are dynamic configuration variables set in the UDF. I used the approach in https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/rest-receiver-adapter-multiple-end-points/qaq-p/12452398 to set this up

The UDF writes logs to PIMON and I can see ALC, STATUS and LMDT being properly derived. However when I run the interface, I get the following mapping error in PIMON 

MP: exception caught with cause com.sap.aii.adapter.rest.ejb.parse.LiteralValueExpectedException: Expected literal value in pattern "https://partnerserver/services/v2_0/stc/{id}{ALC}{STATUS}{LMDT}" at position 45

Any ideas on how to fix this ?

entry.title: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy rules
entry.content:
You might have received email or text notifications about the status of your anticipated delivery from a parcel provider/logistics company. You can navigate to their website and see the basic tracking information of your shipment without logging in. This is a very common in B2C world, but how this is enabled in B2B? A shipper may be managing the transportation and may not provide access to all customers to their own web platform to view detailed status.

The aim of SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace (GTT in short) is to capture, process and store tracking information about tracked business processes. Then, it allows business users to get real-time transparency of the execution of those processes. 

Sending email notification is not new for GTT, but it has some drawbacks,

  • It requires event-to-action script- means business user cannot do it and need support from IT to build and change rules.
  • A GTT user is needed to access the tracking information. By configuring custom roles and assign it to user is one approach used earlier. But managing a user for each customer is not an easy task.

A new feature is available (in test landscape as of now) which eliminate both the drawbacks. Now business user can define rules for notification with an intuitive app with zero coding. GTT now provides public URL by which anyone can access basic tracking information without logging to GTT with a user (the way you access FedEx or UPS tracking link without having a user in their website) Let's see how this is achieved in simple two steps.

1. Configure email notification template

2. Define Email Rules

Step 1. Configure email notification template

First step is to define an email notification template by opening 'Create Notification Template' app in GTT. If you are not able to see this app, then you may be missing role 'ReminderTemplateProcessTemplate' for your user. The app provides a nice MS word like user interface where you can define your template (see below screenshot). You can have placeholders for information from shipment document using ^{<placeholdername>}. Refer SAP Help for other placeholder types. You may use 'Check Processes and Events' app to know all fields available in shipment document including technical fields. In this case, our template is to notify customer if there is a delay reported on the shipment by logistics service provider. Save the template with a name such as 'Delay'.

Step 2: Define Email Rules

After creating email notification template, second step is to define a rule on when the notification must be triggered by GTT. A new button 'Email Rules' is available on Manage Model app for this purpose (as shown in below screenshot). You must need either 'DataModelAdminTemplate' or 'EmailRulesProcessTemplate' roles to view this button. If you are a business user, the second role is appropriate.

By clicking this link will open the rule definition page as shown below. Add a new rule by clicking the '+' button. Provide a Description and make it active.  The first portion of rule defines 'When' it must be triggered. You can pick any planned or unplanned event on the available tracked process. In our case, it is Delay event on a shipment tracked process. So, select "Shipment.Delay" as Current Event and 'Shipment.Shipment' as Correlated Tracked Process. The search help on both fields brings all available values.

Second portion of the rule is to define further conditions based on attributes of the shipment. You can add condition as dynamic attribute value from current event, correlated event or shipment by clicking 'Compared to Model Field Value'. In our case, I want to trigger notification only for road or parcel mode of transport and the estimated arrival time calculated on the delay event is more than 2 hours of the planned arrival time (I don't want GTT bombarded to customer with hundreds of emails for every minute change, but only if estimated arrival is later than 2 hours from planned considering the arrival delay tolerance). So, I define second condition as "MatchedPlannedEvent.estimatedTimestamp is great than MatchedPlannedEvent.plannedBizTsLatest + 2 Hour ". Here in this context, 'Delay' is the current event, and Matched Planned Event must be Arrival (I am not bothered about a delay if it impacts other events than arrival at customer location). To make sure delay is impacting estimated arrival at customer, we add another condition 'MatchedPlannedEvent.locationAltKey is equal to CorrelatedTrackedProcess.arrivalLocationAltKey'.

Thats complete our triggering conditions. Now feed the placeholder we defined at step 1 from email template. Select the email template ID 'Delay' and language as 'English'. Placeholder Name must match exactly same as defined in email template at step 1. Value can be current/related event or from shipment tracked process. You can select any available fields from shipment including custom fields. Here, I used a custom field ZZ_CUSTOMER_COMM_EMAIL to pass customer's email address since this is not passed to GTT by default. You can easily extend GTT extractor to add custom fields- look at my previous blog on how to do it.  Also note that, GTT now provides a publicly accessible URL to every shipment- use attribute 'CorrelatedTrackedProcess.publicLink'. 

By now, our setup is complete, lets test it. Report a delay event manually in Track Shipment app for a road shipment, for testing purpose and provide estimated time of arrival which is +2 hour than planned arrival.

After reporting this event, shipment looks as shown below. Planned arrival is Nov 21 12:00AM CST and estimated is Nov 21 8:25:02AM CST. 

So, our triggering conditions for email met and GTT sends an email to the customer email address specified in the shipment document as shown below.

You can also configure to send Microsoft365 applications like Teams Channel or Teams Chat for any notification (this requires event-to-action scripting, and your organization must allow incoming webhook).

The shipment URL is accessible without a user in GTT, OfCourse it will display only limited information due to privacy reasons, but sufficient for most use cases.

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2411
entry.content:
Thanks to everyone who's turned out for a Sydney SAP Stammtisch in 2024. We wrapped things up with a great event at The Paragon Hotel last Thursday night.  We had about 35 attendees, which is terrific. That seems to be the new normal turnout for us

Simon Kemp joined us once again and it was great that he was able to catch up with lots of old friends and golf buddies

We had a couple of first timers too and they are most welcome

I noted the feedback that it was a little too loud in the pub.  If you have any other feedback please do share it with us (e.g. by commenting here). We are also keen to hear of good venues that could work for a #SAPStammtischSYD

Look out for the next event in early/mid Feb '25.  It will be announced here, on linkedin, via email, and also at bsky.app/profile/mikedoyle.bsky.social

entry.title: Re: Lost SAP Support Notes Access on my S* number after U...
entry.content:
Hi wilbert_sison2,

To unlink your S-user from the linked SAP Universal ID, you have two request options available as described in KBA 2914815 - How to unlink users from an SAP Universal ID or delete the UID.

1.Create a Case:

Create a case for component XX-SER-UID and provide the following:

    • Primary SAP Universal ID email address:
    • Delete entire SAP Universal ID: YES/NO
    • Unlink (S-user ID, P-user ID):
    • Remove email address:
    • Reason for the request:

  • Important: the case must be created by one of the S-users linked to the SAP Universal ID account or by a super, cloud or user admin of the customer number where the primary SAP Universal ID email belongs to

2.Contact Customer Interaction Center (CIC):

If you are unable to create a case, send your request via e-mail to the Customer Interaction Center and provide the following:

    • Primary SAP Universal ID email address:
    • Delete entire SAP Universal ID: YES/NO
    • Unlink (S-user ID, P-user ID):
    • Remove email address:
    • Reason for the request:

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: Re: Bidirectional data replication with SDI
entry.content:
Hi Christian,

SDI does support replication to different target.
For example:
1. Setup DP agent connect to HANA A
2. In HANA A, create two remote sources to connect to HANA B, C
3. Create VT table connect to HANA B, C.(VT_B, VT_C)
4. Setup replication task/flowgraph to do replication from VT_B to VT_C
So you can see in the example above, use VT table as target in your replication, then you can move data to target other than the current hana system.

But keep in mind, performance to other target is not good. But we do provide such function.
In your case. If both the source and target are HANA database, my suggestion is you setup two setup SDI.
Two dp agent connection to two HAHA, within each other setup remote source to connect to the other to replicate data.

Best regads
Helen
